behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about combining multiple signals (commit divergence + age threshold + PR absence), which is a module handoff failure boundary, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: the text does not require 'live repo', real HTTP, or subprocess execution. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because the behavior only states computed conditions ('commits not in main', 'older than configured age threshold', 'no open PR') and does not require real OS/network boundaries. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector + branch metadata provider + PR-state provider using realistic fixtures (including threshold config) and assert stale/no-stale outcomes across edge combinations; verify failures appear when one provider hands incorrect state to detector.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when session bootstrap, staleness detector, and warning renderer are wired together (context can be dropped or mislabeled at boundaries). REAL-INFRA: behavior text says 'session-start setup emits' but does not require real OS/session daemon/network. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because no explicit requirement for real subprocess/HTTP/OS primitives is present; this is local orchestration. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initializer in-process with injected stale branch state and assert emitted warning payload includes actionable fields (branch name, age, ahead count/PR status) and appears only when stale.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicitly a 'local ... function' mapping state to labels, so failure boundary is one function/object. No module handoff, no I/O, no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests over branch-state inputs to assert exact label mapping and deterministic repeatability (same input always same label).

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: GROUNDING-CONTRACT evidence: behavior explicitly says 'against live repo and PR state'. REAL-INFRA: requires real git/repo state and real PR source interaction, which crosses subprocess/OS and likely network boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can pass while hiding failures in real git state reads, branch ancestry resolution, auth/rate-limit/latency, or rendering fed by live fetch results. Lower-level miss-proof: Integration with stubs cannot expose discrepancies between actual repo/PR state retrieval and rendered classification.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo with actual branch divergence and real PR-state source (or real API sandbox) and assert rendered output changes between clean vs stale-no-pr scenarios.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks interaction between branch-state evaluation and PR-status interpretation (open/merged should suppress stale-no-pr), a cross-module rule. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require 'live' or real network; deterministic fixtures for PR states are sufficient for minimum failure detection. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because no explicit live/API requirement appears in this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector with fixture branches and PR states (open, merged, closed-without-merge, none) and assert stale-no-pr is suppressed only for open/merged states.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: GROUNDING-CONTRACT evidence: behavior explicitly targets 'across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change'. REAL-INFRA: these are real VCS history transformations best exercised via actual git operations/subprocess semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocked commit graphs can miss failures in how git actually rewrites ancestry/patch equivalence, causing false stale classification after rewrite/merge. Lower-level miss-proof: Integration fixtures with synthetic hashes may not reproduce real rebase/squash edge semantics that detector logic depends on.
behaviors.test_description: Create real branches in a test repo, perform rebase and squash-merge flows that rewrite commit hashes, then run staleness detection and assert branch is no longer classified stale-no-pr when work is already represented in main/merged history.
